riddles.riddle: I was the world's first computer programmer, having written an algorithm for the Analytical Engine in the mid-1800s. Who am I?
riddles.description: Correct! Ada Lovelace saw the potential for computers to do more than just crunch numbers, envisioning them as tools for creativity and logic.

riddles.riddle: In 1947, a physical moth was found trapped in a relay of the Harvard Mark II computer. This incident popularized what 3-letter term for a technical error?
riddles.description: Exactly! Grace Hopper's team literally 'debugged' the machine by removing the moth and taping it into their logbook.

riddles.riddle: I am the foundational protocol of the World Wide Web, used to transmit data between a client and a server. I usually come before a '://' in your browser.
riddles.description: You've got it. Hypertext Transfer Protocol is the language of the web, allowing us to fetch the very resources you're looking at right now.

riddles.riddle: Created by Linus Torvalds in 2005, I am the most widely used version control system in the world. What is my name?
riddles.description: Spot on. Whether you are branching, merging, or committing, Git is the backbone of modern collaborative software development.

riddles.riddle: I am a simple, logic-based test proposed in 1950 to determine if a machine can exhibit intelligent behavior indistinguishable from a human. What am I called?
riddles.description: Excellent. Alan Turing's 'Imitation Game' remains a philosophical cornerstone in the field of Artificial Intelligence.

riddles.riddle: I am a wrought-iron lattice tower in Paris, originally built for the 1889 World's Fair. What is my name?
riddles.description: Magnifique! You've found the 'Iron Lady.' Fun fact: she was originally intended to be a temporary structure, but her height made her perfect for radio transmissions!

riddles.riddle: I am a massive ancient wall that winds across thousands of miles to protect the northern borders of China. What am I called?
riddles.description: Impressive! You've conquered the longest structure ever built by humans. It is actually a collection of many walls built over several centuries.

riddles.riddle: I am a colossal copper statue standing in New York Harbor, holding a torch high as a symbol of freedom. Who am I?
riddles.description: Excellent! A gift from France to the U.S., she has welcomed millions of people to American shores since 1886. Her official name is 'Liberty Enlightening the World.'

riddles.riddle: Located in Giza, Egypt, I am the oldest and largest of the three pyramids and was the tallest man-made structure for over 3,800 years. What am I?
riddles.description: A monumental achievement! You've solved the mystery of the last remaining wonder of the ancient world, built for the Pharaoh Khufu.

riddles.riddle: What phrase is said when one of us is frustrated?
riddles.description: Ah yes, "Strong deeds, gentle words."... perfect for a state motto, or when you've been slighted.

riddles.riddle: What did Chelsea mistakenly misname for years in Nebraska until she was corrected in Utah?
riddles.description: The poor dear...

riddles.riddle: What is our one, only true regret from our entire marriage?
riddles.description: Worse than He-Who-Must-Not-Be-Named.

riddles.riddle: What activity helped us realize that we are two different people who just do things differently, and that is okay?
riddles.description: For the record, the right way is water first, then toothpaste.

riddles.riddle: What activity helped us realize that I must be cold-blooded?
riddles.description: I think I mostly tend to like warmer showers than you now.

riddles.riddle: What is another term we have used for attractive or appealing?
riddles.description: Boxy is the new sexy.

riddles.riddle: What nickname did I think would be awesome for a son?
riddles.description: Yeah, probably best to wait another generation or two before bringing that one into the mix.

riddles.riddle: What did we think was a bit odd that Kevin and Kristin Newman said they would never do?
riddles.description: I'm glad that what is mine is your and what's yours is mine.

riddles.riddle: Around age 2, what would Gideon say that we perfectly understood, but then again, never really did?
riddles.description: That might be the first thing I ask when I get to heaven.

riddles.riddle: What unappealing food were you glad to choose in the end?
riddles.description: Liverpostej is actually not so bad. Kind of like refried beans.

riddles.riddle: What band name were you surprised, perhaps appalled to learn of?
riddles.description: Never would have guessed that we both like moldy peaches.

riddles.riddle: What was initially perceived as a concern for disaster which quickly was an impressive feat, accomplished by Trevor?
riddles.description: Luckily, I learned that one from my Mother.

riddles.riddle: What was said as a jestful invitation to call calm any anxiousness towards endowment?
riddles.description: No comment.

riddles.riddle: ______, isn't it about...time?
riddles.description: To find what you seek, look beyond the blanked word... Good luck and Happy Mother's Day!
